Community restoration has returned rooms, verandahs and gardens to public view, giving the place the feel of a lived-in archive rather than a sealed display case. It is a gentle stop between Waihi Beach, Athenree and Katikati, but it carries a larger story of migration, adaptation and domestic work.

reviews.summary: Visitors appreciate the volunteer restoration, garden setting and the rare chance to step inside a small late-19th-century Bay of Plenty homestead.

pois.name: Te Puna Quarry Park
pois.short: A former quarry transformed by volunteers into terraced gardens, sculpture paths and harbour viewpoints.
pois.commentary: Te Puna Quarry Park is a landscape of second chances. What was once a scar in the hillside is now layered with palms, bromeliads, sculpture and lookout points, all built through volunteer patience; it is one of Tauranga’s most generous community-made places.

pois.name: Ōtanewainuku Forest
pois.short: A community-protected forest south of Tauranga, known for birdlife, rimu and a lookout climb.
pois.commentary: Ōtanewainuku is a forest with guardians. Community conservation has helped native birds return, and the tracks move through damp green bush toward lookout points where the Bay of Plenty spreads out below; it rewards people who listen as much as look.

pois.name: Onekawa Te Māwhai Regional Park
pois.short: A high coastal park near Ōpōtiki with short walks and sweeping East Coast views.
pois.commentary: Onekawa Te Māwhai is a small park with a big horizon. Tracks climb above the coast near Ōhiwa and Ōpōtiki, looking over beaches, estuary water and headlands; it is an excellent slow-down stop on a journey that too often hurries between towns.

pois.name: TECT All Terrain Park
pois.short: A large outdoor recreation park between Tauranga and Rotorua with bike, horse, motor and walking zones.
pois.commentary: TECT All Terrain Park is built for people who like their outdoors with options. Mountain bikers, horse riders, walkers, motorsport clubs and shooting groups all use different parts of the huge site, making it one of the Bay’s most practical adventure hubs.

pois.name: Pāpāmoa Hills Sunset Walk
pois.short: A popular climb above Tauranga’s coast, especially good in late light over Mauao and the harbour.
pois.commentary: Pāpāmoa Hills earns its local loyalty at sunset. The climb is honest but not epic, and from the top the Bay of Plenty lays itself out in layers: beach, harbour, Mauao, farmland and ranges, all shifting colour as the day drops away.

pois.name: Kohi Point Lookout
pois.short: A Whakatāne lookout on the track between town and Ōhope, with coast and island views.
pois.commentary: Kohi Point is where Whakatāne lifts its eyes to the coast. Whether you tackle the full walkway or just climb to the lookout, the reward is a view that connects river mouth, town, Ōhope Beach and the open Bay of Plenty.

pois.name: Motu Dunes Trail
pois.short: A family-friendly coastal cycle section of the Motu Trails, rolling beside dunes east of Ōpōtiki.
pois.commentary: The Motu Dunes Trail is the easygoing face of the bigger Motu adventure. It follows the coast from Ōpōtiki through dune country, with sea air, open views and enough small rises to keep the ride lively without turning it into a mission.

pois.name: McLaren Falls Park Lake Loop
pois.short: A lakeside park outside Tauranga with easy walks, picnic lawns, kayaking and glowworm evenings nearby.
pois.commentary: McLaren Falls Park is Tauranga’s inland picnic escape: lake edges, old trees, grassy slopes and short walks that do not demand much except time. Come in daylight for the water and shade, or linger into evening when nearby glowworms add a small secret to the park.

pois.short: Thornton Beach is a quieter Eastern Bay coast stop where river-mouth geography, surf and open sand give the landscape a working, local feel.

pois.name: Puketoki Reserve
pois.short: A small native forest reserve near Tauranga with easy loop tracks and family-friendly bush time.
pois.commentary: Puketoki Reserve is the kind of pocket of bush that makes a travel day kinder. The loop is short, shaded and easy to fold around bigger Tauranga plans, but it still gives the pleasure of birdsong, fern edges and a feeling of stepping under a green roof.

pois.name: Ohope Scenic Reserve
pois.short: Coastal forest tracks above Ohope Beach, with birds, pohutukawa and outlooks over the eastern Bay.
pois.commentary: Ohope Scenic Reserve sits just above the beach, but the mood changes quickly once the track enters the forest. The coast is still close enough to smell, while nikau, pohutukawa and birds carry the walk into a cooler, older layer of the Eastern Bay.
